buckets.name: professional
buckets.definition: Work and building: code, software, dev tools, infrastructure, AI/ML models and agents, startups, product, growth, markets, investing, economy, crypto, jobs, hiring, and workplace/career.
buckets.replyStance: Engage as a peer: add a concrete experience, a gotcha, or a sharper question — never generic agreement. Only where I have real hands-on ground; skip hype. Never investment advice or predictions.

buckets.name: personal-social
buckets.definition: Human, everyday, and cultural life: the author's own daily life, family, food, outings and milestones; health, fitness and wellness; memes, jokes and banter; and movies, music, books, sports and pop culture.
buckets.replyStance: Warm, specific, human. Celebrate wins, share genuine reactions, be light back when it's a joke — never unsolicited advice, never prescribe. Only reply if I add warmth or something actually funny.

buckets.name: ideas
buckets.definition: Ideas worth thinking about: research, science, space, psychology, history and explainers; and faith, meditation, mindset, meaning and philosophical reflection.
buckets.replyStance: Curious questions beat hot takes; engage the idea and amplify what is genuinely interesting. Respectful of faith — engage the thought, never debate someone's beliefs.

buckets.name: civic
buckets.definition: Public affairs: elections, policy, government and partisan takes; activism, justice and societal causes; and non-partisan breaking news and current events.
buckets.replyStance: Default skip unless I genuinely know the topic and have real substance to add. Never dunk, never pile on, never performative agreement.

buckets.name: other
buckets.definition: Mandatory fallback: thin @-replies, greetings, conversational fragments, and anything topicless or that fits no bucket above. Use when unsure — never force-fit.
buckets.replyStance: Judge on its own merits; if it is unclear why it would deserve a reply, skip.
